articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Robust Digital Privacy Rights in Landmark Verdict
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial verdict on the scope of digital privacy, affirming it as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
• The ruling came in a challenge against certain provisions of the proposed 'National Digital Identity Framework 2.0', which aimed to integrate various government services.
• The Court emphasized the need for a balanced approach, allowing data collection for legitimate state interests while mandating strict anonymization and robust consent mechanisms.
• It directed the government to establish an independent data protection authority with significant powers to oversee compliance and address grievances.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for India's digital economy, surveillance policies, and future legislative frameworks on data governance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's role in upholding fundamental rights.
Article 21: Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Puttaswamy Judgement (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right.
Concept of 'National Digital Identity Framework'.
Role of a Data Protection Authority.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests in national security/public welfare.
Analyze the implications of the verdict on government surveillance and data collection policies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's digital economy in light of robust data protection laws.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in safeguarding fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest reforms for effective implementation of data protection laws and the establishment of an independent regulatory body.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block with digital data streams in the background, symbolizing a legal verdict on digital privacy.
articles.trivia: India is home to the world's largest biometric ID system, Aadhaar, which also faced legal challenges regarding privacy.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Stability and Green Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The 14th India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing security cooperation, including maritime domain awareness and defense technology exchanges.
• Both nations stressed collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, particularly in green hydrogen, renewable energy, and semiconductors.
• India sought greater Japanese investment in its infrastructure projects, especially in the National Industrial Corridors Programme.
• The dialogue also covered regional security challenges, including developments in the South China Sea and implications for global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan bilateral relations: key areas of cooperation.
Significance of Indo-Pacific region.
Green Hydrogen as a focus area for bilateral cooperation.
National Industrial Corridors Programme.
Importance of semiconductors in global economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan relations in shaping the Indo-Pacific order.
Discuss the potential for India-Japan collaboration in achieving energy security and climate goals.
Evaluate the role of economic cooperation in strengthening the India-Japan partnership.
Examine how India and Japan can collectively address regional security challenges.
Suggest measures to deepen defense and technological collaboration between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a high-level diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably contributing to the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance, balancing persistent retail inflation concerns with the need to support economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY26 at 7.1%, while retail inflation is expected to average around 5.2% in the coming quarters.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and volatile food prices as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate.
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Inflation target set by RBI.
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Relationship between inflation, interest rates, and economic growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing India's economic challenges.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative policy measures the RBI could consider to achieve its objectives.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with financial charts in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016, aiming to bring transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Accelerates, Targets 5 Million Tonne Production by 2030
articles.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is making significant strides, with several pilot projects achieving operational status across different states.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced that the mission is on track to achieve its target of producing 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• Financial incentives and production-linked schemes have attracted substantial private investment, particularly in electrolyser manufacturing.
• Focus areas include green hydrogen applications in refining, fertilizers, steel, and transportation sectors, with pilot projects demonstrating viability.
• Challenges remain in scaling up infrastructure, reducing production costs, and establishing a robust supply chain for hydrogen transportation and storage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: Target of National Green Hydrogen Mission (5 MMT by 2030).
Key sectors for green hydrogen application.
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production.
SIGHT Programme.
Difference between green, grey, and blue hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various industrial sectors and the transportation landscape.
Suggest measures to overcome technological and economic barriers to accelerate green hydrogen development.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating zero carbon emissions.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focusing on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, targeting a lunar sample return.
• This mission aims to collect lunar soil and rock samples from a previously unexplored region of the Moon and bring them back to Earth for scientific analysis.
• Chandrayaan-4 will involve a complex multi-module architecture, including an orbiter, lander, rover, and an ascent module for sample return.
• The mission builds upon the successes and learnings from Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, particularly in soft landing technologies.
• Scientists anticipate that the retrieved samples will provide invaluable insights into the Moon's geological evolution and the early solar system.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Objective of Chandrayaan-4 mission (Lunar Sample Return).
Components of a typical lunar sample return mission (orbiter, lander, rover, ascent module).
Previous Chandrayaan missions (Chandrayaan-1, 2, 3).
ISRO's capabilities in space exploration.
Significance of lunar samples for scientific research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for advancing scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in designing and executing a successful lunar sample return mission.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its position among leading spacefaring nations.
Examine the socio-economic benefits and strategic implications of India's ambitious space programs.
Suggest how international collaborations can further enhance India's lunar exploration efforts.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 lander collecting samples on the lunar surface.
articles.trivia: Only the US, Russia (then USSR), and China have successfully brought lunar samples back to Earth.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Unified Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Information Technology presented its report, advocating for a unified and robust cybersecurity framework.
• The report highlighted vulnerabilities in critical information infrastructure (CII) across sectors like finance, energy, and telecom.
• It recommended establishing a single nodal agency with executive powers to coordinate national cybersecurity efforts, replacing fragmented approaches.
• The panel stressed the need for mandatory cybersecurity audits, enhanced training for personnel, and greater public-private partnership in threat intelligence sharing.
• Concerns were raised regarding the shortage of skilled cybersecurity professionals and the slow adoption of advanced security technologies.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Definition of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Role of NCIIPC and CERT-In.
Importance of cybersecurity for national security.
Concept of a unified cybersecurity framework.
Challenges in cybersecurity workforce development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the increasing threats to India's critical information infrastructure and their potential impact on national security.
Discuss the advantages and challenges of implementing a unified cybersecurity framework in India.
Evaluate the current institutional mechanisms for cybersecurity in India and suggest areas for improvement.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in strengthening India's cybersecurity posture.
Propose strategies to address the shortage of skilled cybersecurity professionals in the country.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a network of interconnected systems, symbolizing cybersecurity for critical infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's National Cyber Security Strategy was first formulated in 2013, with updates periodically discussed to address evolving threats.

articles.headline: Centre Launches New Urban Flood Resilience Initiative for Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative' targeting 20 major Indian cities prone to severe flooding.
• The initiative focuses on integrated water management, upgrading drainage infrastructure, early warning systems, and adoption of nature-based solutions.
• It emphasizes data-driven planning using advanced hydrological models and satellite imagery for real-time flood monitoring.
• Capacity building for urban local bodies and community engagement in flood preparedness are also key components.
• The project aims to reduce economic losses and human casualties caused by increasingly frequent and intense urban flood events.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Causes of urban flooding in India.
Components of urban flood resilience (drainage, early warning, nature-based solutions).
Role of Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Examples of nature-based solutions for flood control.
Data-driven planning in disaster management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major causes and consequences of urban flooding in India's metropolitan cities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current urban planning and infrastructure in mitigating flood risks.
Analyze the role of technology and nature-based solutions in enhancing urban flood resilience.
Examine the challenges in implementing integrated urban flood management strategies.
Suggest policy recommendations for sustainable urban development to address future climate risks.
articles.imageDescription: A city skyline with a graphic overlay showing water flow and drainage systems, symbolizing urban flood management.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a clean waterway, became a major contributor to urban flooding due to encroachment and pollution.

articles.headline: India's G20 Sherpa Attends Preparatory Summit, Focus on Global South Priorities
articles.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa participated in the preparatory summit in Brazil, setting the agenda for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• India strongly advocated for amplifying the voice of the Global South on critical issues like climate finance, food security, and digital public infrastructure.
• Discussions included reforms of multilateral development banks, sustainable development goals (SDGs) acceleration, and equitable energy transitions.
• India reiterated its commitment to the LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) movement and promoting sustainable consumption patterns globally.
• The summit aimed to build consensus on key deliverables ahead of the main event later next year.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Current G20 Presidency (Brazil).
Role of a G20 Sherpa.
Key priorities of the Global South in G20.
Concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
LiFE movement and its objectives.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and influence in the G20 forum, particularly in advocating for the Global South.
Discuss the significance of digital public infrastructure in achieving sustainable development goals in developing nations.
Evaluate the challenges in reforming multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of the Global South.
Examine how the LiFE movement can contribute to global efforts towards sustainable consumption.
Suggest strategies for India to further strengthen its leadership in global forums like the G20.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of G20 member country flags with a globe in the center, representing global cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing about two-thirds of the world's population and 85% of global GDP.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, curriculum reforms, and the integration of vocational education.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development in rural areas, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The review emphasized the need for greater state cooperation and resource allocation to fully realize the policy's objectives.
• Recommendations include accelerating the development of national credit frameworks and strengthening higher education regulatory bodies.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key features of NEP 2020.
Objectives of foundational literacy and numeracy.
Concept of vocational education integration.
Challenges in education sector (teacher training, infrastructure).
Role of National Credit Framework.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in achieving a knowledge-based society in India.
Examine the role of technology and digital learning in bridging educational disparities, especially in rural areas.
Evaluate the importance of teacher training and capacity building for the success of education reforms.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education for all sections of society under NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom, with educational symbols like books and a globe, representing the National Education Policy.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986, which had been in place for 34 years.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty, Resolve Minor Disputes
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual technical talks under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) in Islamabad.
• The Permanent Indus Commission discussed several technical issues related to the design of run-of-the-river hydroelectric projects on the Western rivers.
• Both sides reported constructive engagement and successfully resolved minor technical disagreements regarding water flow measurements and data sharing.
• India reiterated its commitment to the spirit of the IWT while asserting its right to utilize its share of water for legitimate purposes.
• The talks are a crucial mechanism for maintaining peaceful water-sharing arrangements between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Year of Indus Water Treaty.
Rivers allocated to India and Pakistan.
Role of Permanent Indus Commission.
Concept of 'run-of-the-river' projects.
World Bank's role in IWT.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance and continued relevance of the Indus Water Treaty in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in maintaining water-sharing agreements amidst geopolitical tensions.
Evaluate India's rights and obligations under the IWT regarding the utilization of river waters.
Examine the role of technical dialogue and the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving water disputes.
Suggest measures for strengthening transboundary water cooperation in South Asia.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with India and Pakistan's borders, symbolizing water sharing.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, signed in 1960, was brokered by the World Bank and is considered one of the most successful water treaties in the world.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog belonging to the genus 'Nyctibatrachus' (Night Frog) in the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
• The newly identified species is characterized by its unique vocalizations and specific habitat requirements within evergreen forests.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect such fragile ecosystems from habitat loss and climate change.
• Genetic analysis confirmed its distinctiveness from known species, adding to the rich faunal diversity of the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
Ecosystems and their functioning.
prelimsPoints: Location of Western Ghats (biodiversity hotspot).
Characteristics of amphibians (indicator species).
Importance of biodiversity hotspots.
Threats to biodiversity in Western Ghats.
UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and its role in maintaining ecosystem balance.
Analyze the major threats to biodiversity in India, with a specific focus on the Western Ghats region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies and protected area networks in safeguarding endemic species.
Examine the impact of climate change on amphibian populations and broader biodiversity.
Suggest policy interventions and community-based approaches for enhanced biodiversity conservation.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered frog species in a lush green forest environment.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Rural Livelihoods Mission Empowers Women SHGs, Boosts Rural Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) reported significant achievements in empowering women Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
• Over 10 million women have been mobilized into SHGs, with access to credit exceeding Rs. 5 lakh crore, leading to diverse livelihood activities.
• The mission has focused on capacity building, skill development, and market linkages for SHG products, enhancing income generation.
• Success stories include women-led enterprises in agriculture, handicrafts, food processing, and digital services, contributing to local economies.
• Challenges include ensuring quality control, scaling up market access, and addressing regional disparities in SHG formation and success rates.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: Objectives of DAY-NRLM.
Role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in rural development.
Impact of microfinance on women empowerment.
Key sectors where SHGs operate.
Government initiatives for rural livelihoods.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) as a tool for women empowerment and poverty alleviation in rural India.
Analyze the achievements and challenges of the National Rural Livelihoods Mission in fostering sustainable rural livelihoods.
Evaluate the effectiveness of financial inclusion strategies in boosting the rural economy through SHGs.
Examine how market linkages and skill development can further enhance the impact of SHG initiatives.
Suggest policy measures to address regional disparities and ensure equitable growth of SHGs across the country.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural women engaged in a craft activity, symbolizing Self-Help Group initiatives.
articles.trivia: The concept of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) originated in India in the early 1980s, primarily driven by NGOs.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Mission Attracts Global Players, Boosts Domestic Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• India's ambitious Semiconductor Mission has begun to yield results, with several global semiconductor giants announcing plans for fabrication units and design centers.
• The production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes and a supportive policy environment have been instrumental in attracting foreign direct investment.
• The focus is on establishing a complete semiconductor ecosystem, from design and fabrication to assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP).
• This push aims to reduce India's reliance on imported chips and position the country as a significant player in the global semiconductor supply chain.
• Challenges include securing advanced technology transfer, developing a skilled workforce, and ensuring consistent power and water supply for fabs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of India's Semiconductor Mission.
Role of PLI schemes in manufacturing.
Components of semiconductor ecosystem (design, fab, ATMP).
Strategic importance of semiconductors.
Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in critical sectors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of domestic semiconductor manufacturing for India's economic growth and national security.
Discuss the effectiveness of government policies, such as PLI schemes, in attracting investment in the semiconductor sector.
Examine the challenges India faces in building a robust semiconductor ecosystem and achieving technological self-reliance.
Evaluate the potential impact of a thriving semiconductor industry on India's digital economy and global supply chain position.
Suggest measures to develop a skilled workforce and ensure sustainable growth of the semiconductor sector in India.
articles.imageDescription: A microchip with intricate circuitry, representing semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.trivia: Semiconductors are often referred to as the 'brains' of modern electronics, powering everything from smartphones to advanced defense systems.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Climate Change Impact on India's Agricultural Productivity
articles.explainer:
• A new report by ICAR (Indian Council of Agricultural Research) details the increasing impact of climate change on India's major crop yields.
• Extreme weather events, including prolonged droughts, erratic rainfall, and heatwaves, are leading to significant agricultural losses.
• The report projects a potential reduction of 10-25% in yields of key crops like wheat, rice, and maize by 2040 under current emission scenarios.
• It recommends urgent adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices, drought-resistant crop varieties, and improved irrigation technologies.
• The study also calls for better weather forecasting and early warning systems for farmers to mitigate risks effectively.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Land reforms in India.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
prelimsPoints: Impact of climate change on specific crops (wheat, rice, maize).
Role of ICAR in agricultural research.
Key climate-resilient agricultural practices.
Importance of early warning systems for farmers.
Different types of extreme weather events.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted impact of climate change on India's agricultural sector and its implications for food security.
Discuss the various strategies and technologies available for promoting climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and research institutions like ICAR in mitigating climate risks for farmers.
Examine the socio-economic consequences of reduced agricultural productivity due to climate change.
Suggest a comprehensive policy framework for adapting Indian agriculture to the changing climate scenario.
articles.imageDescription: A parched agricultural field under a scorching sun, with a farmer looking on, symbolizing drought's impact.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat, making its agricultural sector highly vulnerable to climate shifts.

articles.headline: Centre Announces Enhanced Funding for Judicial Infrastructure Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Law Ministry announced a significant increase in budgetary allocation for the development of judicial infrastructure across states.
• The funds will be utilized for constructing new court buildings, modernizing existing facilities, and upgrading digital infrastructure for e-courts.
• The initiative aims to address the long-standing issue of inadequate infrastructure, which contributes to pendency of cases and judicial delays.
• Emphasis is also placed on providing better amenities for judges, lawyers, and litigants, including accessibility for persons with disabilities.
• States are urged to utilize the funds efficiently and submit utilization certificates promptly to ensure continuous support.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
keyTerms: National Judicial Infrastructure Authority of India (NJIAI)
prelimsPoints: Causes of judicial delays in India.
Role of e-courts in modernizing judiciary.
Importance of judicial infrastructure.
Ministry responsible for judicial infrastructure.
Concept of 'Access to Justice'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of robust judicial infrastructure in ensuring timely and accessible justice in India.
Discuss how inadequate infrastructure contributes to judicial pendency and its impact on the rule of law.
Evaluate the potential of e-courts and digital infrastructure in transforming the Indian judiciary.
Examine the challenges in implementing judicial infrastructure projects across diverse states.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to strengthen judicial infrastructure and improve the efficiency of the justice delivery system.
articles.imageDescription: A modern court building facade with 'Justice' written, symbolizing judicial infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The e-Courts Mission Mode Project aims to transform the Indian judiciary by integrating technology into court processes.

articles.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth, Focus on Deep Tech and Rural Innovation
articles.explainer:
• India's startup ecosystem recorded another year of strong growth, with increased funding rounds and a surge in new registrations, particularly in Tier 2 and 3 cities.
• Government initiatives like 'Startup India' and 'Fund of Funds for Startups' have played a crucial role in fostering an enabling environment.
• A notable trend is the rising prominence of 'deep tech' startups in AI, blockchain, and advanced manufacturing, attracting significant venture capital.
• Efforts are also being made to promote rural innovation, leveraging local talent and resources to address grassroots challenges.
• Challenges include navigating regulatory complexities, access to patient capital for deep tech, and attracting top-tier talent from global markets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of 'Startup India' initiative.
Concept of 'deep tech' startups.
Role of venture capital in startup funding.
Significance of startups for economic growth and employment.
Growth of startup ecosystem in Tier 2/3 cities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth of India's startup ecosystem and its impact on economic development.
Discuss the role of government policies and funding mechanisms in fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
Examine the opportunities and challenges for 'deep tech' startups in India and their potential to drive technological advancement.
Evaluate the significance of rural innovation and its contribution to inclusive growth and job creation.
Suggest measures to further strengthen India's startup ecosystem, including regulatory reforms and access to finance.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing innovation.
articles.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: Goa Celebrates 64th Liberation Day with Patriotic Fervor
articles.explainer:
• Goa is celebrating its 64th Liberation Day, commemorating the day in 1961 when it was liberated from 450 years of Portuguese rule.
• Chief Minister addressed a state-level function, highlighting Goa's journey of progress and its unique cultural identity.
• Various cultural programs, parades, and felicitations of freedom fighters were organized across the state.
• The day serves as a reminder of India's efforts to integrate all its territories post-independence and the struggles of Goan freedom fighters.
• Discussions also touched upon the challenges of sustainable tourism and infrastructure development in the state.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Date of Goa's liberation (December 19, 1961).
Colonial power in Goa (Portuguese).
Military operation for liberation ('Operation Vijay').
Years of Portuguese rule.
Other former Portuguese territories in India (Daman & Diu).
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and significance of Goa's liberation in the broader narrative of India's post-independence territorial integration.
Examine the methods employed by India to liberate Goa from Portuguese rule and international reactions to it.
Analyze the unique cultural and political identity of Goa and its evolution since liberation.
Evaluate the challenges and achievements of Goa in its journey as an Indian state.
Discuss the importance of remembering such historical events for national identity and unity.
articles.imageDescription: Indian flag hoisted in Goa with a historical monument in the background, symbolizing liberation.
articles.trivia: Goa was the last territory in India to be freed from foreign rule.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Promote Women in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a comprehensive initiative aimed at increasing the participation and retention of women in STEM fields.
• The program includes scholarships, mentorship opportunities, flexible work policies, and grants for women researchers and entrepreneurs.
• It seeks to address the gender gap in STEM, particularly at senior leadership and research positions, by creating a more inclusive ecosystem.
• Collaborations with educational institutions and industry partners are planned to provide skill development and career advancement opportunities.
• The initiative acknowledges the significant contributions of women scientists and aims to harness their full potential for national development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Full form of STEM.
Government initiatives for women in science.
Challenges faced by women in STEM careers.
Importance of gender diversity in research.
Role of Ministry of Science & Technology.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current status of women's participation in STEM fields in India and the reasons for the existing gender gap.
Discuss the socio-economic benefits of increasing women's representation in science and technology for national development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes and policies aimed at promoting women in STEM.
Examine the role of educational institutions, industry, and societal attitudes in fostering an inclusive environment for women scientists.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including policy and cultural changes, to encourage and retain women in STEM careers.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of women scientists in a laboratory setting, symbolizing women in STEM.
articles.trivia: Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields (Physics and Chemistry).

articles.headline: Largest Floating Solar Power Park Commissioned in Southern India
articles.explainer:
• India's largest floating solar power park, with a capacity of 200 MW, was commissioned in a reservoir in one of the Southern Indian states.
• The project utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology and aims to contribute significantly to the state's renewable energy targets.
• Floating solar panels offer advantages such as reduced land requirement, lower evaporation rates from water bodies, and improved panel efficiency due to cooling effect.
• This development is part of India's broader strategy to diversify its renewable energy portfolio and achieve its ambitious climate goals.
• Environmental impact assessments were conducted to ensure minimal disruption to aquatic ecosystems and local biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Benefits of floating solar power plants.
India's renewable energy targets.
Concept of photovoltaic technology.
Environmental impact assessment (EIA).
Advantages over ground-mounted solar panels.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential and advantages of floating solar power technology for India's renewable energy transition.
Analyze the environmental and economic benefits of large-scale floating solar projects.
Examine the challenges involved in the development and operation of floating solar parks in India.
Evaluate the role of such projects in helping India achieve its climate change mitigation targets.
Suggest policy measures to promote the widespread adoption of innovative renewable energy solutions.
articles.imageDescription: A vast expanse of solar panels floating on a reservoir, reflecting the sky.
articles.trivia: Floating solar farms can produce more electricity than land-based ones because water cools the panels, making them more efficient.

highlights: Supreme Court upholds robust digital privacy rights, mandating strict anonymization and independent data protection authority.
India-Japan Strategic Dialogue strengthens Indo-Pacific stability and green technology cooperation, including green hydrogen and semiconductors.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for fifth consecutive review, balancing inflation concerns with economic growth support.
National Green Hydrogen Mission accelerates, on track for 5 million tonne production by 2030, attracting significant private investment.
ISRO ramps up preparations for Chandrayaan-4, an ambitious lunar sample return mission to collect and analyze lunar soil and rock samples.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court verdict on digital privacy, consider the following statements:
1. The Court affirmed digital privacy as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
2. It directed the establishment of an independent data protection authority with significant oversight powers.
3. The verdict allows unrestricted data collection by the state for national security purposes without any checks.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Supreme Court's ruling emphasized digital privacy as part of Article 21, building on the Puttaswamy judgment. Statement 2 is correct: The Court directed the government to establish an independent data protection authority. Statement 3 is incorrect: The ruling emphasized a balanced approach, allowing data collection for legitimate state interests but mandating strict anonymization and robust consent mechanisms, not unrestricted collection without checks.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a stated focus area for collaboration between India and Japan in the recent Strategic Dialogue?
options: (c) Joint military exercises in the Arctic region
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Joint military exercises in the Arctic region
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article mentions enhancing security cooperation including maritime domain awareness, collaboration in green hydrogen, renewable energy, and semiconductors. Joint military exercises in the Arctic region are not mentioned as a focus area for India-Japan collaboration in the context of the Indo-Pacific Strategic Dialogue.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission targets an annual production of 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity generated from fossil fuels.
3. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme is a key component of this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission aims for 5 MMT annual production by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable electricity, not fossil fuels. Statement 3 is correct: SIGHT Programme provides financial incentives and is a key component.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of ISRO's upcoming Chandrayaan-4 mission?
options: (a) To establish a permanent human outpost on the Moon.
(b) To launch a new lunar orbiter for high-resolution mapping.
(c) To conduct a lunar sample return mission from an unexplored region.
(d) To test advanced propulsion systems for interplanetary travel.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To conduct a lunar sample return mission from an unexplored region.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article clearly states that Chandrayaan-4 mission is targeting a lunar sample return, aiming to collect lunar soil and rock samples from a previously unexplored region of the Moon and bring them back to Earth for scientific analysis.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for implementing the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)?
options: (c) Ministry of Women and Child Development
(d) Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsMocks.explanation: The DAY-NRLM falls under the purview of the Ministry of Rural Development, which is responsible for various rural development and livelihood generation schemes in India.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court verdict on digital privacy reaffirms the judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights in the digital age. Critically analyze the implications of this judgment on government policies, individual liberties, and the future of data governance in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC verdict and its link to Article 21 and Puttaswamy. Discuss implications on government policies (data collection, surveillance, National Digital Identity Framework), emphasizing the need for balance. Address impact on individual liberties (privacy, autonomy). Discuss the future of data governance, including the role of a data protection authority and legislative changes. Conclude with the importance of robust data protection for democratic values.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is a cornerstone of its energy transition strategy. Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals. What are the key challenges in scaling up its production and adoption, and how can they be addressed? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment. Infrastructure: Energy. Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by defining green hydrogen and its importance for India's net-zero targets and reducing fossil fuel dependence. Detail its role in energy security (reducing imports) and climate goals (decarbonization of hard-to-abate sectors). Enumerate challenges: high production costs, infrastructure for storage/transport, technology scaling, water availability, skilled workforce. Suggest solutions: R&D, PLI schemes, international collaboration, policy support, green financing, and pilot projects.

mainsMocks.question: The growing impact of climate change on India's agricultural productivity poses a significant threat to food security. Analyze the various ways climate change affects Indian agriculture and critically evaluate the effectiveness of current adaptation strategies. Suggest comprehensive measures for building a climate-resilient agricultural sector. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country. Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation. Disaster and disaster management. Food security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce climate change impact on agriculture (extreme weather, erratic monsoon, heatwaves, pest outbreaks) and its threat to food security. Discuss current adaptation strategies (drought-resistant seeds, micro-irrigation, crop diversification, weather advisories), evaluating their reach and effectiveness. Propose comprehensive measures: R&D in climate-smart crops, improved forecasting, robust insurance, community-based adaptation, nature-based solutions, policy support for sustainable practices, and investment in rural infrastructure.

quickBytes.text: Goa Liberation Day is celebrated on December 19th, marking its freedom from Portuguese rule in 1961.
The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty, including digital privacy.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy, resulting in zero carbon emissions.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India is responsible for determining the benchmark interest rates.
India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, after the US and China.
The Indus Water Treaty (1960) allocates the waters of the Beas, Ravi, and Sutlej rivers to India.
The e-Courts Mission Mode Project aims to integrate technology for efficient justice delivery in India.
Semiconductors are essential components in almost all modern electronic devices, often called the 'brains' of electronics.
The LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) movement promotes sustainable consumption patterns globally.

GS1.headline: Goa Celebrates 64th Liberation Day with Patriotic Fervor
GS1.explainer:
• Goa is celebrating its 64th Liberation Day, commemorating the day in 1961 when it was liberated from 450 years of Portuguese rule.
• Chief Minister addressed a state-level function, highlighting Goa's journey of progress and its unique cultural identity.
• Various cultural programs, parades, and felicitations of freedom fighters were organized across the state.
• The day serves as a reminder of India's efforts to integrate all its territories post-independence and the struggles of Goan freedom fighters.
• Discussions also touched upon the challenges of sustainable tourism and infrastructure development in the state.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Date of Goa's liberation (December 19, 1961).
Colonial power in Goa (Portuguese).
Military operation for liberation ('Operation Vijay').
Years of Portuguese rule.
Other former Portuguese territories in India (Daman & Diu).
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and significance of Goa's liberation in the broader narrative of India's post-independence territorial integration.
Examine the methods employed by India to liberate Goa from Portuguese rule and international reactions to it.
Analyze the unique cultural and political identity of Goa and its evolution since liberation.
Evaluate the challenges and achievements of Goa in its journey as an Indian state.
Discuss the importance of remembering such historical events for national identity and unity.
GS1.imageDescription: Indian flag hoisted in Goa with a historical monument in the background, symbolizing liberation.
GS1.trivia: Goa was the last territory in India to be freed from foreign rule.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Robust Digital Privacy Rights in Landmark Verdict
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial verdict on the scope of digital privacy, affirming it as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
• The ruling came in a challenge against certain provisions of the proposed 'National Digital Identity Framework 2.0', which aimed to integrate various government services.
• The Court emphasized the need for a balanced approach, allowing data collection for legitimate state interests while mandating strict anonymization and robust consent mechanisms.
• It directed the government to establish an independent data protection authority with significant powers to oversee compliance and address grievances.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for India's digital economy, surveillance policies, and future legislative frameworks on data governance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's role in upholding fundamental rights.
Article 21: Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Puttaswamy Judgement (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right.
Concept of 'National Digital Identity Framework'.
Role of a Data Protection Authority.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests in national security/public welfare.
Analyze the implications of the verdict on government surveillance and data collection policies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's digital economy in light of robust data protection laws.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in safeguarding fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest reforms for effective implementation of data protection laws and the establishment of an independent regulatory body.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block with digital data streams in the background, symbolizing a legal verdict on digital privacy.
GS2.trivia: India is home to the world's largest biometric ID system, Aadhaar, which also faced legal challenges regarding privacy.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Stability and Green Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• The 14th India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing security cooperation, including maritime domain awareness and defense technology exchanges.
• Both nations stressed collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, particularly in green hydrogen, renewable energy, and semiconductors.
• India sought greater Japanese investment in its infrastructure projects, especially in the National Industrial Corridors Programme.
• The dialogue also covered regional security challenges, including developments in the South China Sea and implications for global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan bilateral relations: key areas of cooperation.
Significance of Indo-Pacific region.
Green Hydrogen as a focus area for bilateral cooperation.
National Industrial Corridors Programme.
Importance of semiconductors in global economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan relations in shaping the Indo-Pacific order.
Discuss the potential for India-Japan collaboration in achieving energy security and climate goals.
Evaluate the role of economic cooperation in strengthening the India-Japan partnership.
Examine how India and Japan can collectively address regional security challenges.
Suggest measures to deepen defense and technological collaboration between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a high-level diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably contributing to the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

GS2.headline: India's G20 Sherpa Attends Preparatory Summit, Focus on Global South Priorities
GS2.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa participated in the preparatory summit in Brazil, setting the agenda for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• India strongly advocated for amplifying the voice of the Global South on critical issues like climate finance, food security, and digital public infrastructure.
• Discussions included reforms of multilateral development banks, sustainable development goals (SDGs) acceleration, and equitable energy transitions.
• India reiterated its commitment to the LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) movement and promoting sustainable consumption patterns globally.
• The summit aimed to build consensus on key deliverables ahead of the main event later next year.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Current G20 Presidency (Brazil).
Role of a G20 Sherpa.
Key priorities of the Global South in G20.
Concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
LiFE movement and its objectives.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and influence in the G20 forum, particularly in advocating for the Global South.
Discuss the significance of digital public infrastructure in achieving sustainable development goals in developing nations.
Evaluate the challenges in reforming multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of the Global South.
Examine how the LiFE movement can contribute to global efforts towards sustainable consumption.
Suggest strategies for India to further strengthen its leadership in global forums like the G20.
GS2.imageDescription: A collage of G20 member country flags with a globe in the center, representing global cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing about two-thirds of the world's population and 85% of global GDP.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, curriculum reforms, and the integration of vocational education.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development in rural areas, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The review emphasized the need for greater state cooperation and resource allocation to fully realize the policy's objectives.
• Recommendations include accelerating the development of national credit frameworks and strengthening higher education regulatory bodies.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key features of NEP 2020.
Objectives of foundational literacy and numeracy.
Concept of vocational education integration.
Challenges in education sector (teacher training, infrastructure).
Role of National Credit Framework.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in achieving a knowledge-based society in India.
Examine the role of technology and digital learning in bridging educational disparities, especially in rural areas.
Evaluate the importance of teacher training and capacity building for the success of education reforms.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education for all sections of society under NEP 2020.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom, with educational symbols like books and a globe, representing the National Education Policy.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986, which had been in place for 34 years.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty, Resolve Minor Disputes
GS2.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual technical talks under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) in Islamabad.
• The Permanent Indus Commission discussed several technical issues related to the design of run-of-the-river hydroelectric projects on the Western rivers.
• Both sides reported constructive engagement and successfully resolved minor technical disagreements regarding water flow measurements and data sharing.
• India reiterated its commitment to the spirit of the IWT while asserting its right to utilize its share of water for legitimate purposes.
• The talks are a crucial mechanism for maintaining peaceful water-sharing arrangements between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Year of Indus Water Treaty.
Rivers allocated to India and Pakistan.
Role of Permanent Indus Commission.
Concept of 'run-of-the-river' projects.
World Bank's role in IWT.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance and continued relevance of the Indus Water Treaty in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in maintaining water-sharing agreements amidst geopolitical tensions.
Evaluate India's rights and obligations under the IWT regarding the utilization of river waters.
Examine the role of technical dialogue and the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving water disputes.
Suggest measures for strengthening transboundary water cooperation in South Asia.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with India and Pakistan's borders, symbolizing water sharing.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, signed in 1960, was brokered by the World Bank and is considered one of the most successful water treaties in the world.

GS2.headline: Rural Livelihoods Mission Empowers Women SHGs, Boosts Rural Economy
GS2.explainer:
• The Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) reported significant achievements in empowering women Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
• Over 10 million women have been mobilized into SHGs, with access to credit exceeding Rs. 5 lakh crore, leading to diverse livelihood activities.
• The mission has focused on capacity building, skill development, and market linkages for SHG products, enhancing income generation.
• Success stories include women-led enterprises in agriculture, handicrafts, food processing, and digital services, contributing to local economies.
• Challenges include ensuring quality control, scaling up market access, and addressing regional disparities in SHG formation and success rates.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: Objectives of DAY-NRLM.
Role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in rural development.
Impact of microfinance on women empowerment.
Key sectors where SHGs operate.
Government initiatives for rural livelihoods.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) as a tool for women empowerment and poverty alleviation in rural India.
Analyze the achievements and challenges of the National Rural Livelihoods Mission in fostering sustainable rural livelihoods.
Evaluate the effectiveness of financial inclusion strategies in boosting the rural economy through SHGs.
Examine how market linkages and skill development can further enhance the impact of SHG initiatives.
Suggest policy measures to address regional disparities and ensure equitable growth of SHGs across the country.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural women engaged in a craft activity, symbolizing Self-Help Group initiatives.
GS2.trivia: The concept of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) originated in India in the early 1980s, primarily driven by NGOs.

GS2.headline: Centre Announces Enhanced Funding for Judicial Infrastructure Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Law Ministry announced a significant increase in budgetary allocation for the development of judicial infrastructure across states.
• The funds will be utilized for constructing new court buildings, modernizing existing facilities, and upgrading digital infrastructure for e-courts.
• The initiative aims to address the long-standing issue of inadequate infrastructure, which contributes to pendency of cases and judicial delays.
• Emphasis is also placed on providing better amenities for judges, lawyers, and litigants, including accessibility for persons with disabilities.
• States are urged to utilize the funds efficiently and submit utilization certificates promptly to ensure continuous support.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
keyTerms: National Judicial Infrastructure Authority of India (NJIAI)
prelimsPoints: Causes of judicial delays in India.
Role of e-courts in modernizing judiciary.
Importance of judicial infrastructure.
Ministry responsible for judicial infrastructure.
Concept of 'Access to Justice'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of robust judicial infrastructure in ensuring timely and accessible justice in India.
Discuss how inadequate infrastructure contributes to judicial pendency and its impact on the rule of law.
Evaluate the potential of e-courts and digital infrastructure in transforming the Indian judiciary.
Examine the challenges in implementing judicial infrastructure projects across diverse states.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to strengthen judicial infrastructure and improve the efficiency of the justice delivery system.
GS2.imageDescription: A modern court building facade with 'Justice' written, symbolizing judicial infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: The e-Courts Mission Mode Project aims to transform the Indian judiciary by integrating technology into court processes.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Promote Women in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM)
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a comprehensive initiative aimed at increasing the participation and retention of women in STEM fields.
• The program includes scholarships, mentorship opportunities, flexible work policies, and grants for women researchers and entrepreneurs.
• It seeks to address the gender gap in STEM, particularly at senior leadership and research positions, by creating a more inclusive ecosystem.
• Collaborations with educational institutions and industry partners are planned to provide skill development and career advancement opportunities.
• The initiative acknowledges the significant contributions of women scientists and aims to harness their full potential for national development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Full form of STEM.
Government initiatives for women in science.
Challenges faced by women in STEM careers.
Importance of gender diversity in research.
Role of Ministry of Science & Technology.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current status of women's participation in STEM fields in India and the reasons for the existing gender gap.
Discuss the socio-economic benefits of increasing women's representation in science and technology for national development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes and policies aimed at promoting women in STEM.
Examine the role of educational institutions, industry, and societal attitudes in fostering an inclusive environment for women scientists.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including policy and cultural changes, to encourage and retain women in STEM careers.
GS2.imageDescription: A diverse group of women scientists in a laboratory setting, symbolizing women in STEM.
GS2.trivia: Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields (Physics and Chemistry).

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance, balancing persistent retail inflation concerns with the need to support economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY26 at 7.1%, while retail inflation is expected to average around 5.2% in the coming quarters.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and volatile food prices as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate.
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Inflation target set by RBI.
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Relationship between inflation, interest rates, and economic growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing India's economic challenges.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative policy measures the RBI could consider to achieve its objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with financial charts in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016, aiming to bring transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Accelerates, Targets 5 Million Tonne Production by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is making significant strides, with several pilot projects achieving operational status across different states.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced that the mission is on track to achieve its target of producing 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• Financial incentives and production-linked schemes have attracted substantial private investment, particularly in electrolyser manufacturing.
• Focus areas include green hydrogen applications in refining, fertilizers, steel, and transportation sectors, with pilot projects demonstrating viability.
• Challenges remain in scaling up infrastructure, reducing production costs, and establishing a robust supply chain for hydrogen transportation and storage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: Target of National Green Hydrogen Mission (5 MMT by 2030).
Key sectors for green hydrogen application.
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production.
SIGHT Programme.
Difference between green, grey, and blue hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various industrial sectors and the transportation landscape.
Suggest measures to overcome technological and economic barriers to accelerate green hydrogen development.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating zero carbon emissions.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focusing on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, targeting a lunar sample return.
• This mission aims to collect lunar soil and rock samples from a previously unexplored region of the Moon and bring them back to Earth for scientific analysis.
• Chandrayaan-4 will involve a complex multi-module architecture, including an orbiter, lander, rover, and an ascent module for sample return.
• The mission builds upon the successes and learnings from Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, particularly in soft landing technologies.
• Scientists anticipate that the retrieved samples will provide invaluable insights into the Moon's geological evolution and the early solar system.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Objective of Chandrayaan-4 mission (Lunar Sample Return).
Components of a typical lunar sample return mission (orbiter, lander, rover, ascent module).
Previous Chandrayaan missions (Chandrayaan-1, 2, 3).
ISRO's capabilities in space exploration.
Significance of lunar samples for scientific research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for advancing scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in designing and executing a successful lunar sample return mission.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its position among leading spacefaring nations.
Examine the socio-economic benefits and strategic implications of India's ambitious space programs.
Suggest how international collaborations can further enhance India's lunar exploration efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 lander collecting samples on the lunar surface.
GS3.trivia: Only the US, Russia (then USSR), and China have successfully brought lunar samples back to Earth.

GS3.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Unified Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Information Technology presented its report, advocating for a unified and robust cybersecurity framework.
• The report highlighted vulnerabilities in critical information infrastructure (CII) across sectors like finance, energy, and telecom.
• It recommended establishing a single nodal agency with executive powers to coordinate national cybersecurity efforts, replacing fragmented approaches.
• The panel stressed the need for mandatory cybersecurity audits, enhanced training for personnel, and greater public-private partnership in threat intelligence sharing.
• Concerns were raised regarding the shortage of skilled cybersecurity professionals and the slow adoption of advanced security technologies.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Definition of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Role of NCIIPC and CERT-In.
Importance of cybersecurity for national security.
